The key features—fluctuating cognition, visual hallucinations, parkinsonian tremor, and autonomic dysfunction—are hallmark signs of **Lewy body dementia (LBD)**. - **Fluctuating mental status**: A core feature of LBD, characterized by sudden changes in attention and alertness. - **Visual hallucinations**: Often vivid and detailed, commonly involving people or animals (e.g., "snakes"), which are a cardinal symptom of LBD. - **Tremor and parkinsonism**: Motor symptoms like tremor and rigidity are typical in LBD, though less prominent than in Parkinson’s disease. - **Autonomic dysfunction**: Orthostatic hypotension, sweating, and other autonomic signs are common in LBD due to involvement of the autonomic nervous system.
